enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based project management software with Gantt charts, task automation, time tracking, and 50-plus integrations for teams planning and tracking work.
enrichment.starting_price: Free for up to 5 users; paid plans from 4 dollars per user per month billed annually
enrichment.headquarters: Chennai, India (Zoho Corporation; US headquarters in Austin, Texas)
enrichment.seo_title: Zoho Projects Review: Features, Pricing and Alternatives (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what Zoho Projects offers: Gantt charts, task automation, time tracking, and pricing plans from free to Ultimate. Compare features and find alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zoho Projects is a cloud-based project management platform built by Zoho Corporation, an Indian multinational software company founded in 1996 and headquartered in Chennai with a US base in Austin, Texas. Zoho Projects helps teams plan, track, and collaborate on projects through Gantt charts that visualize task dependencies and schedule variance, time tracking with built-in timers for billable and non-billable hours, and drag-and-drop task automation that streamlines recurring workflows. The platform supports extensive customization, including custom fields, layouts, statuses, and blueprints tailored to a team's process, and it integrates with more than 50 third-party applications such as Slack, GitHub, Jira, and Dropbox, alongside native connections to other Zoho apps like Zoho Invoice and Zoho CRM. It is available on web, iOS, and Android, enabling distributed and remote teams to manage work from anywhere. Zoho reports that Zoho Projects hosts more than 4.5 million projects and holds ISO 27001 certification for enterprise-grade security. Pricing follows a freemium model: a Free plan supports up to 5 users, 3 projects, and 5GB of storage; the Premium plan (around 4 to 5 dollars per user per month) adds unlimited projects, custom views, and task blueprints; the Enterprise plan (around 9 to 10 dollars per user per month) is the most popular tier and adds custom domains, single sign-on, and higher workflow automation limits; and the Ultimate plan (around 15 dollars per user per month) adds expanded storage, more read-only users, and the highest workflow execution caps. Annual billing offers savings over monthly billing, and all paid tiers include a 15-day free trial with no credit card required. Zoho Projects is positioned as a budget-friendly alternative to tools like Asana, Monday.com, and Microsoft Project, particularly for organizations already using other Zoho products, since deep native integration with the wider Zoho One suite (CRM, Desk, Books, Invoice) is one of its strongest differentiators. It suits small to mid-sized teams and project managers who need Gantt-based planning and automation without the cost of enterprise project portfolio management software, though very large enterprises with complex resource management needs may find dedicated PPM tools more robust.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zoho Projects
paragraphs: Zoho Projects is a web-based project management application from Zoho Corporation, designed to help teams plan, execute, and track projects collaboratively. It combines task management, Gantt charts, time tracking, and workflow automation in a single platform aimed at dynamic and often globally distributed teams.

As part of the broader Zoho software suite, Zoho Projects is built to work natively with other Zoho applications such as Zoho CRM, Zoho Books, and Zoho Invoice, making it a natural fit for organizations already standardized on Zoho tools, while still supporting 50-plus external integrations including Slack, GitHub, Jira, and Dropbox.
The platform emphasizes flexibility through custom fields, statuses, layouts, and task blueprints so teams can adapt the software to their existing processes rather than forcing a rigid methodology.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Gantt charts provide visual timelines showing task dependencies and highlighting gaps between planned and actual progress, helping project managers spot schedule risk early.
Time tracking includes built-in timers for logging billable and non-billable hours, with the ability to generate invoices directly through integration with Zoho Invoice.
Task automation uses a drag-and-drop workflow builder (Blueprints) to standardize repetitive processes, reducing manual handoffs between team members.
Reporting and dashboards give visibility into resource utilization, task status, and project health, while mobile apps for iOS and Android let teams manage projects remotely.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zoho Projects offers a Free plan for up to 5 users, 3 projects, and 5GB of storage with no credit card required.

Paid plans include Premium (around 4 to 5 dollars per user per month), Enterprise (around 9 to 10 dollars per user per month, the most popular tier), and Ultimate (around 15 dollars per user per month), each unlocking more storage, automation executions, and administrative controls such as custom domains and single sign-on.
Annual billing reduces the effective per-user cost compared with monthly billing, and every paid plan comes with a 15-day free trial.

features.title: Gantt charts
features.description: Visual timelines that map task dependencies and flag deviations between planned and actual progress.

features.title: Time tracking
features.description: Built-in timers for billable and non-billable hours with automatic invoice generation via Zoho Invoice.

features.title: Task automation (Blueprints)
features.description: Drag-and-drop workflow builder to automate recurring, multi-step processes.

features.title: Custom fields and layouts
features.description: Configurable statuses, fields, and views to match a team's existing workflow.

features.title: 50-plus integrations
features.description: Native connections to Slack, GitHub, Jira, Dropbox, and other Zoho apps like CRM and Books.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: iOS and Android apps for managing tasks and tracking time on the go.

features.title: Resource and workload reports
features.description: Dashboards showing task status, workload distribution, and project health.

features.title: Enterprise security
features.description: ISO 27001-certified infrastructure with SSO and custom domain support on higher plans.

pros: Generous free tier for small teams (up to 5 users)
Deep native integration with the wider Zoho One suite
Competitive pricing compared to Asana, Monday.com, and similar tools
Strong Gantt chart and time tracking functionality out of the box
15-day free trial on all paid plans with no credit card required
cons: Interface can feel dated compared to newer project management tools
Advanced automation and reporting are locked behind higher-priced tiers
Read-only user limits and workflow execution caps vary by plan and can require upgrades for larger teams
Best value is realized when paired with other Zoho apps, less compelling as a standalone tool

faqs.question: Is Zoho Projects free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho Projects offers a Free plan for up to 5 users, 3 projects, and 5GB of storage with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Zoho Projects cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start around 4 to 5 dollars per user per month for Premium and scale up to around 15 dollars per user per month for the Ultimate plan, with lower rates on annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Projects have Gantt charts?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gantt charts are a core feature, showing task dependencies and highlighting discrepancies between planned and actual progress.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Projects integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports over 50 integrations including Slack, GitHub, Jira, and Dropbox, plus native integration with other Zoho applications.

faqs.question: Can Zoho Projects track billable hours?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes built-in time tracking with timers for billable and non-billable hours and can generate invoices via Zoho Invoice.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app for Zoho Projects?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho Projects has mobile apps for both iOS and Android.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Premium and Enterprise plans?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds features like custom domains, single sign-on, more read-only users, and higher workflow automation limits compared to Premium.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Projects offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include a 15-day free trial with no credit card required.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based applicant tracking system and recruitment CRM for staffing agencies and in-house HR teams, with job board distribution and AI candidate matching.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans from approximately 25 dollars per recruiter per month billed annually
enrichment.headquarters: Chennai, India (Zoho Corporation; US headquarters in Austin, Texas)
enrichment.seo_title: Zoho Recruit Review: Features, Pricing and Editions (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore Zoho Recruit's applicant tracking features, Corporate HR and Staffing Agency editions, AI tools, and pricing plans from free to Enterprise.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zoho Recruit is a cloud-based applicant tracking system (ATS) and recruitment CRM built by Zoho Corporation, founded in 1996 and headquartered in Chennai, India, with a US headquarters in Austin, Texas. The platform manages the full hiring lifecycle from job posting and sourcing through interviewing, offer, and onboarding, without requiring on-premise installation. It distributes job postings to more than 75 job boards simultaneously, tracks candidates through customizable pipeline stages with analytics, and supports virtual recruitment workflows including remote interviews, background checks, and assessments. Zoho Recruit ships as two distinct product editions targeting different buyers: the Corporate HR edition, for in-house talent acquisition teams hiring for their own organization, and the Staffing Agency edition, for recruiting firms and search agencies placing candidates with external clients. The Staffing Agency edition adds agency-specific functionality such as client and vendor management, client portals, and substantially higher active-job limits (up to hundreds of open jobs versus 10 to 20 on Corporate HR plans). As of late 2025, Zoho added AI-powered features through its Zia engine, including a Sourcing Bot, Screening Bot, and AI candidate matching, available on the Professional and Enterprise tiers. The product integrates with more than 50 third-party tools including Slack, LinkedIn, Outlook, and other Zoho applications like Zoho CRM. A genuinely free plan exists but is limited to one active job, 256MB of storage, and no resume parsing, AI, or integrations, making it suitable only for solo recruiters with a single opening. Paid plans are priced per recruiter per month and billed annually at a discount versus monthly billing: Standard plans start around 25 dollars per user per month, Professional (Staffing Agency only) around 50 dollars, and Enterprise around 75 dollars, with common add-ons like video interviews and client portals pushing real costs higher. Zoho emphasizes flexible, pay-for-what-you-need pricing that scales for organizations of any size, and offers a 15-day free trial with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zoho Recruit
paragraphs: Zoho Recruit is a cloud-based recruitment platform that combines applicant tracking system (ATS) functionality with candidate relationship management (CRM), helping organizations manage the entire talent pipeline from sourcing through onboarding.

The product is split into two editions built for different buyers: Corporate HR, for internal talent acquisition teams, and Staffing Agency, for recruiting and search firms placing candidates with external clients. Each edition has its own plan structure and feature set at similar price points.
Zoho Recruit is part of the larger Zoho software suite and integrates natively with Zoho CRM and other Zoho apps, while also connecting to external tools like Slack, LinkedIn, and Outlook.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Job posting automation lets recruiters publish openings to 75-plus job boards at once, reducing manual posting work across multiple sites.
Candidate tracking follows applicants through customizable pipeline stages with analytics on time-to-fill and source effectiveness.
AI tools powered by Zoho's Zia engine, including Sourcing Bot, Screening Bot, and candidate matching, were added in late 2025 on Professional and Enterprise tiers.
Staffing Agency-specific tools include client and vendor portals, formatted resumes, and multi-org support for agencies managing multiple client relationships.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zoho Recruit offers a limited free plan (one active job, 256MB storage, no AI or resume parsing) alongside paid Standard, Professional, and Enterprise tiers priced per recruiter per month.
Corporate HR plans run Standard around 25 dollars and Enterprise around 50 dollars per user per month billed annually, with fewer active job limits than the Staffing Agency edition.

Staffing Agency plans run Standard around 25 dollars, Professional around 50 dollars, and Enterprise around 75 dollars per user per month billed annually, with monthly billing typically about 20 percent higher and optional add-ons like video interviews and client portals adding to total cost.

features.description: Connects with Slack, LinkedIn, Outlook, Zoho CRM, and other business tools.

features.description: iOS and Android apps for managing candidates and communications on the go.

pros: Two purpose-built editions for in-house HR versus staffing agencies
Extensive job board distribution network (75-plus boards)
AI-powered candidate matching and screening on higher tiers
Native integration with Zoho CRM and the broader Zoho suite
15-day free trial with no credit card required
cons: Free plan is very limited (one active job, no AI or resume parsing)
AI features are restricted to Professional and Enterprise tiers
Add-ons like video interviews and client portals can push real costs well above sticker price
Two-edition structure (Corporate HR vs Staffing Agency) can be confusing to first-time buyers

faqs.question: Is Zoho Recruit free?
faqs.answer: Zoho Recruit has a free plan, but it is limited to one active job, 256MB of storage, and no resume parsing, AI, or integrations.

faqs.question: How much does Zoho Recruit cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans typically start around 25 dollars per recruiter per month for Standard and scale up to around 75 dollars per recruiter per month for Enterprise, billed annually.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Corporate HR and Staffing Agency editions?
faqs.answer: Corporate HR is built for in-house teams hiring for their own company with fewer active job limits, while Staffing Agency adds client and vendor management tools and supports far more active jobs for external placements.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Recruit have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho added AI tools through its Zia engine in late 2025, including Sourcing Bot, Screening Bot, and AI candidate matching, available on Professional and Enterprise tiers.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Recruit post to job boards?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can distribute job postings to more than 75 job boards simultaneously.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Recruit integrate with Zoho CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates natively with Zoho CRM and other Zoho applications, plus external tools like Slack, LinkedIn, and Outlook.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Zoho Recruit?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho Recruit offers a 15-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who uses Zoho Recruit?
faqs.answer: Both in-house HR teams building internal talent pipelines and staffing or recruiting agencies placing candidates with external clients.

enrichment.short_description: Social media management platform for scheduling posts, monitoring brand mentions, and reporting analytics across channels like Instagram, LinkedIn, and TikTok.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans from approximately 15 dollars per month
enrichment.headquarters: Chennai, India (Zoho Corporation; US headquarters in Austin, Texas)
enrichment.seo_title: Zoho Social Review: Features, Pricing and Agency Plans (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Compare Zoho Social's scheduling, monitoring, and analytics features against its Standard, Professional, Premium, and Agency pricing plans.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zoho Social is a social media management platform from Zoho Corporation, founded in 1996 and headquartered in Chennai, India, with a US headquarters in Austin, Texas. It gives individual creators, small teams, marketing agencies, and large enterprises a unified interface for planning, scheduling, publishing, monitoring, and analyzing content across X (formerly Twitter), Instagram, Facebook, Google Business Profile, LinkedIn, YouTube, Pinterest, Mastodon, TikTok, Threads, Bluesky, WhatsApp, and Telegram, depending on plan tier. Core capabilities include content scheduling with SmartQ, a predictive feature that suggests optimal posting times based on audience activity; social listening tools that track brand mentions, hashtags, and relevant conversations to surface leads and monitor sentiment; and visual analytics and reporting covering engagement, traffic sources, and click distribution. The platform supports team collaboration through content approval workflows, draft review, and message discussions, and integrates with Zoho CRM, Zoho Desk, and Canva. Zoho Social's pricing is structured around two product lines: Business plans (Free, Standard, Professional, Premium) for individual brands and small teams, and Agency plans (Agency, Agency Plus) for marketing agencies managing many client brands. The Free plan covers 1 brand across 6 channels for 1 team member with basic scheduling and an image editor. Standard costs approximately 15 dollars per month (around 10 dollars per month billed annually) for 1 brand across up to 11 channels, adding livestreaming, bulk scheduling, and RSS feed monitoring. Professional (around 40 dollars per month) adds SmartQ, content approval workflows, and custom reports. Premium (around 65 dollars per month) adds CRM integrations, lead generation tools, UTM tracking, and supports 3 team members across up to 14 channels including YouTube and WhatsApp. Agency plans start around 320 dollars per month for 10 brands and scale to Agency Plus at around 460 dollars per month for 20 brands, both including client portals, branded client logins, and scheduled reporting. Annual billing saves up to 33 percent versus monthly billing, and additional brands or team members can be added to most plans as paid add-ons.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zoho Social
paragraphs: Zoho Social is a social media management platform that lets businesses, marketers, and agencies plan, schedule, publish, and monitor content across multiple social channels from a single dashboard.

It is designed to serve a wide range of users, from individual creators and small teams managing one brand, to marketing agencies coordinating dozens of client accounts through dedicated Agency plans.
As part of the Zoho suite, Zoho Social integrates with Zoho CRM and Zoho Desk to connect social engagement with sales and support workflows, plus Canva for design.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SmartQ uses predictive analysis of audience activity to recommend the best times to publish content for maximum engagement.
Social monitoring tracks brand mentions, hashtags, and relevant keywords across supported channels, helping teams catch conversations and potential leads in real time.
Visual reports and custom analytics cover engagement metrics, follower growth, traffic sources, and click distribution.
Collaboration tools include content approval workflows, draft review, and team discussions, while Agency plans add client portals and branded client logins.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zoho Social's Free plan covers 1 brand and 6 channels for 1 user with basic scheduling tools.

Business plans scale from Standard (around 15 dollars per month) through Professional (around 40 dollars per month) to Premium (around 65 dollars per month), adding more channels, team members, SmartQ, approval workflows, and CRM integrations at each tier.

Agency plans start around 320 dollars per month for 10 brands (Agency) and go up to around 460 dollars per month for 20 brands (Agency Plus), both bundling client management features; annual billing saves up to 33 percent.

features.description: Connects with Zoho CRM and Zoho Desk to link social activity to sales and support.

features.description: Upload and schedule content in bulk with a centralized media library, from the Standard plan up.

Purpose-built Agency tier with client portals and branded logins
SmartQ predictive scheduling helps optimize post timing
Native integration with Zoho CRM and Zoho Desk
Broad channel coverage including newer platforms like TikTok, Threads, and Bluesky
cons: Higher-tier features like SmartQ and CRM integration require Professional or Premium plans
Agency plans are priced significantly higher than most competitors' entry tiers
Additional brands and team members are paid add-ons on most plans
Some advanced channels (YouTube, WhatsApp) are limited to Premium and above

faqs.question: Is Zoho Social free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho Social offers a free plan for 1 brand across 6 channels with basic scheduling and one team member.

faqs.question: How much does Zoho Social cost?
faqs.answer: Paid Business plans range from around 15 dollars per month (Standard) to around 65 dollars per month (Premium), while Agency plans start around 320 dollars per month.

faqs.question: What is SmartQ in Zoho Social?
faqs.answer: SmartQ is a predictive scheduling feature that recommends optimal posting times based on audience activity, available from the Professional plan up.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Social support TikTok?
faqs.answer: Yes, TikTok is included among the channels supported on Standard plans and above.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Social have an agency plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, dedicated Agency and Agency Plus plans support 10 and 20 brands respectively, with client portals and branded client logins.

faqs.question: Does Zoho Social integrate with Zoho CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, CRM integration is available starting on the Premium plan, linking social engagement to sales pipelines.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Zoho Social?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoho Social offers a 15-day free trial with no credit card required on paid plans.

faqs.question: How many channels can Zoho Social manage per brand?
faqs.answer: Coverage ranges from 6 channels on the Free plan up to 13-14 channels including YouTube and WhatsApp on the Premium plan.

enrichment.short_description: Video conferencing and unified communications platform for meetings, webinars, team chat, and AI-assisted collaboration, used by businesses worldwide.
enrichment.starting_price: Free for basic meetings; Pro plan from approximately 13.33 dollars per user per month billed annually
enrichment.seo_title: Zoom Review: Video Conferencing Features and Pricing (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore Zoom's video meeting features, AI Companion tools, and pricing plans from free Basic to Business Plus and Enterprise tiers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zoom is a video conferencing and unified communications platform developed by Zoom Video Communications, founded in 2011 by former Cisco WebEx engineer Eric Yuan and headquartered in San Jose, California. Zoom's core product, launched publicly in 2013, provides high-definition video and audio meetings, screen sharing, and virtual backgrounds, and has grown into a broader Zoom Workplace suite that bundles meetings, team chat, whiteboard, and AI-powered features under one platform. Zoom Workplace includes AI Companion, a generative AI assistant that provides meeting summaries, smart chat replies, and note-taking across the product line at no additional cost on paid plans. The platform serves consumers, small businesses, and large enterprises, and became a household name during the 2020 shift to remote work, remaining one of the most widely used video communications tools globally. Zoom's plans are structured as: Basic, a free tier limited to 40-minute group meetings and up to 100 participants; Pro, priced around 13.33 dollars per user per month billed annually (16.99 dollars billed monthly), which removes the 40-minute cap for meetings up to 30 hours and adds cloud recording and admin controls; Business, around 18.33 dollars per user per month annually (21.99 dollars monthly), which supports larger organizations with single sign-on, managed domains, and up to 300 participants; and Business Plus, around 29 dollars per user per month annually, which bundles in Zoom Phone and Workspace Reservation features. An Enterprise tier is available with custom pricing for large organizations needing unlimited cloud storage, dedicated customer success support, and higher participant caps. Zoom also offers standalone add-on products including Zoom Phone (cloud calling), Zoom Webinars, Zoom Events, Zoom Contact Center, and Zoom Rooms for conference room hardware. Zoom Video Communications is a publicly traded company (NASDAQ: ZM) with roughly 5,000 to 10,000 employees and reported annual revenue in the multi-billion-dollar range.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zoom
paragraphs: Zoom is a video conferencing and unified communications platform built by Zoom Video Communications, founded in 2011 by Eric Yuan and headquartered in San Jose, California. Its core video meeting product launched publicly in 2013 and became one of the most widely adopted communication tools in the world.

Beyond video calls, Zoom has expanded into a broader Zoom Workplace suite that includes team chat, whiteboarding, scheduling, and Zoom Phone for cloud calling, positioning itself as an all-in-one collaboration platform rather than a single-purpose meeting app.
Zoom serves everyone from individual freelancers hosting free one-on-one calls to large enterprises running company-wide webinars, virtual events, and contact centers on the platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: HD video and audio meetings support screen sharing, virtual backgrounds, breakout rooms, and cloud or local recording.
AI Companion, Zoom's generative AI assistant, provides automatic meeting summaries, smart chat replies, and follow-up task extraction across supported plans.
Zoom Team Chat and Whiteboard extend collaboration beyond live meetings for asynchronous work.
Zoom Rooms and Zoom Phone extend the platform into physical conference rooms and cloud telephony, respectively, as add-on products.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zoom's Basic plan is free and supports meetings up to 100 participants with a 40-minute cap on group calls.

Pro (around 13.33 dollars per user per month annually) removes the time cap for meetings up to 30 hours; Business (around 18.33 dollars per user per month annually) adds SSO and supports up to 300 participants; and Business Plus (around 29 dollars per user per month annually) bundles in Zoom Phone and Workspace Reservation.
Enterprise pricing is custom and includes unlimited cloud storage and dedicated support for large organizations.

features.title: HD video and audio meetings
features.description: Core video conferencing with screen sharing, breakout rooms, and virtual backgrounds.

features.title: AI Companion
features.description: Generative AI assistant offering meeting summaries, smart replies, and task extraction.

features.description: Record meetings to the cloud (paid plans) or locally, with searchable transcripts.

features.description: Persistent messaging and channels integrated with the meetings platform.

features.title: Webinars and virtual events
features.description: Large-scale broadcast tools for hosting webinars and events, sold as add-ons.

features.title: Admin and security controls
features.description: Single sign-on, managed domains, and meeting security controls like waiting rooms and passcodes.

features.title: Zoom Rooms hardware integration
features.description: Turns physical conference rooms into managed video meeting spaces.

pros: Free Basic plan usable for unlimited one-on-one calls and 40-minute group meetings
AI Companion included at no extra cost on paid plans
Widely adopted, making it easy for external participants to join without installation friction
Reliable call quality and broad device and OS support
Scales from solo users to large enterprise deployments with dedicated Enterprise support
cons: Free plan caps group meetings at 40 minutes
Advanced features like SSO and larger participant caps require Business tier or above
Add-on products (Phone, Webinars, Events, Contact Center) increase total cost beyond base meeting plans
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires a custom sales quote

faqs.question: Is Zoom free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoom's Basic plan is free and supports meetings for up to 100 participants, though group meetings are capped at 40 minutes.

faqs.question: How much does Zoom Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Zoom Pro costs around 13.33 dollars per user per month billed annually, or 16.99 dollars per user per month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What is Zoom AI Companion?
faqs.answer: AI Companion is Zoom's generative AI assistant that provides meeting summaries, smart chat replies, and task extraction, included on paid plans at no extra cost.

faqs.question: How many people can join a Zoom meeting?
faqs.answer: The Basic and Pro plans support up to 100 participants, Business supports up to 300, and higher participant capacities are available through add-ons or Enterprise plans.

faqs.question: Who founded Zoom?
faqs.answer: Zoom was founded in 2011 by Eric Yuan, a former Cisco WebEx engineer, and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

faqs.question: Is Zoom Phone included in Zoom's video plans?
faqs.answer: No, Zoom Phone is a separate cloud calling product, though it is bundled into the Business Plus plan.

faqs.question: Does Zoom offer webinars?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoom Webinars and Zoom Events are separate add-on products for large-scale broadcasts and virtual events.

faqs.question: Is Zoom publicly traded?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoom Video Communications trades on NASDAQ under the ticker ZM.

use_cases.title: Remote team meetings
use_cases.description: Distributed teams hold daily standups, all-hands, and one-on-ones over HD video with recording and transcripts.

use_cases.title: Client and sales calls
use_cases.description: Sales and client-facing teams use screen sharing and recording to run demos and client meetings.

use_cases.title: Large-scale webinars and events
use_cases.description: Marketing and events teams broadcast webinars and virtual events to large external audiences using Zoom Webinars and Events add-ons.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based business phone system (VoIP) from Zoom offering metered or unlimited calling plans, auto-attendants, call queues, and AI call summaries.
enrichment.starting_price: From approximately 10.50 dollars per user per month billed annually
enrichment.seo_title: Zoom Phone Review: VoIP Pricing and Features (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Zoom Phone's cloud calling plans work, including Metered, Unlimited, and Global Select pricing, auto-attendants, and AI call features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zoom Phone is the cloud business phone (VoIP) product from Zoom Video Communications, the video conferencing company founded in 2011 by Eric Yuan and headquartered in San Jose, California. While it shares infrastructure and branding with Zoom's flagship video meeting product, Zoom Phone is a distinct, separately priced offering focused on replacing traditional business phone systems with cloud-based calling, SMS, and voicemail. It provides features such as auto-attendants, multi-level call routing, call queues, voicemail transcription, and integration with the wider Zoom Workplace suite including Team Chat and Meetings. As part of Zoom's broader AI Companion rollout, Zoom Phone includes AI-powered post-call summaries, task extraction, and voicemail prioritization on eligible plans. Zoom Phone is sold both as a standalone product and bundled into higher Zoom Workplace tiers. Standalone plans include US and Canada Metered, starting around 10.50 dollars per user per month billed annually, which charges pay-as-you-go rates for outbound calls and suits organizations with low call volume; US and Canada Unlimited, around 16 dollars per user per month annually (18 dollars month-to-month), which adds unlimited domestic calling, advanced call queue management, and multi-level auto-attendant customization; and Global Select, around 20 dollars per user per month annually (25 dollars month-to-month), which provides phone numbers and unlimited calling across a large list of supported countries for international teams. Zoom also bundles Zoom Phone into Workplace plans: Pro Plus, billed around 20.50 dollars per user per month annually, adds AI Companion call transcription and 10GB of cloud recording storage, while Business Plus, around 24.50 dollars per user per month annually, supports up to 250 users with expanded meeting capacity and native CRM automation for platforms like Salesforce. An add-on Power Pack, around 25 dollars per user per month, unlocks real-time queue analytics, wallboard monitoring, and a desktop receptionist console for high-volume call centers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zoom Phone
paragraphs: Zoom Phone is Zoom's cloud-based business phone system, built to replace traditional on-premise PBX hardware with VoIP calling delivered through the same infrastructure that powers Zoom Meetings.

It is a separate, separately priced product from Zoom's core video conferencing plans, though it can be bundled into higher Zoom Workplace tiers like Business Plus for organizations that want unified video, chat, and calling under one subscription.

Zoom Phone targets businesses of all sizes looking to modernize their phone systems, from small teams needing simple metered calling to large enterprises requiring global numbers and advanced call center features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Auto-attendants and multi-level call routing direct incoming calls without a live receptionist, while call queues manage high call volumes for support and sales teams.
AI Companion features bring automatic post-call summaries, task extraction, and voicemail prioritization to eligible Zoom Phone plans.
Global Select plans provide international phone numbers and unlimited calling across supported countries, simplifying global team communications.
The optional Power Pack add-on delivers real-time queue analytics, wallboard monitoring, and a desktop receptionist console for contact center-style operations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Standalone Zoom Phone plans include US and Canada Metered (around 10.50 dollars per user per month annually, pay-as-you-go outbound), US and Canada Unlimited (around 16 dollars per user per month annually), and Global Select (around 20 dollars per user per month annually) for international calling.

Zoom Phone is also bundled into Zoom Workplace tiers: Pro Plus (around 20.50 dollars per user per month annually) and Business Plus (around 24.50 dollars per user per month annually), which combine calling with video meetings, chat, and CRM automation.
An optional Power Pack add-on (around 25 dollars per user per month) adds advanced call center features like queue analytics and a receptionist console.

faqs.question: Is Zoom Phone the same as Zoom Meetings?
faqs.answer: No, Zoom Phone is a separate cloud business phone (VoIP) product from Zoom Video Communications, sold independently or bundled into higher Zoom Workplace plans.

faqs.question: How much does Zoom Phone cost?
faqs.answer: Standalone plans start around 10.50 dollars per user per month for Metered calling and go up to around 20 dollars per user per month for Global Select, all billed annually.

faqs.question: Does Zoom Phone support international calling?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Global Select plan provides international phone numbers and unlimited calling across a large list of supported countries.

faqs.question: Does Zoom Phone include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, eligible plans include AI Companion features like post-call summaries, task extraction, and voicemail prioritization.

faqs.question: Can Zoom Phone be bundled with Zoom Meetings?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zoom Workplace Business Plus and Pro Plus tiers bundle Zoom Phone together with video meetings and team chat.

faqs.question: What is the Zoom Phone Power Pack?
faqs.answer: It is an add-on, around 25 dollars per user per month, that unlocks real-time queue analytics, wallboard monitoring, and a desktop receptionist console.

faqs.question: Does Zoom Phone integrate with CRM tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, native CRM automation for platforms like Salesforce is available on the Business Plus tier.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Metered and Unlimited Zoom Phone plans?
faqs.answer: Metered charges pay-as-you-go rates per outbound call, while Unlimited includes unlimited domestic calling plus advanced call queue and auto-attendant features.

enrichment.short_description: B2B go-to-market intelligence platform providing company and contact data, sales automation, and intent signals for sales, marketing, and recruiting teams.
enrichment.starting_price: Custom annual contracts; SalesOS plans commonly cited from approximately 15,000 dollars per year
enrichment.seo_title: ZoomInfo Review: B2B Data Platform Features and Pricing (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn what ZoomInfo (GTM) offers for B2B contact data, sales intelligence, and go-to-market automation, plus how its custom annual pricing works.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ZoomInfo is a B2B go-to-market intelligence platform, unrelated to the video conferencing company Zoom despite the similar name and separate parent companies. ZoomInfo traces its roots to Eliyon Technologies, founded in 2000, and took its current form after DiscoverOrg acquired and rebranded as ZoomInfo in 2019; the company is headquartered in Vancouver, Washington, and is publicly traded, having changed its NASDAQ ticker from ZI to GTM in May 2025 to reflect its positioning as a go-to-market platform rather than just a contact database. The core product combines a large B2B contact and company database with AI-driven insights, intent data, and workflow automation to help sales, marketing, and recruiting teams find, engage, and convert prospects. Its flagship SalesOS product includes contact and company search, sales engagement and automation, conversation intelligence (via its Chorus acquisition), and workflow tools, while additional modules cover marketing intent data, account-based marketing, and a Copilot AI layer for prioritizing accounts and generating outreach content. ZoomInfo sells exclusively through custom annual contracts with no public self-serve pricing or monthly billing; third-party pricing guides commonly cite SalesOS Professional starting around 14,995 dollars per year, Advanced around 24,995 dollars per year, and Elite at 39,995 dollars per year and above, with per-seat add-ons of roughly 1,500 to 2,500 dollars per user per year on top of the base contract. Additional modules like intent data, ABM, Copilot, and Chorus conversation intelligence can push total annual spend well beyond the base package price, and most sources estimate realistic costs for a mid-sized sales team in the 25,000 to 60,000 dollar range annually. ZoomInfo reported record revenue of approximately 1.25 billion dollars in 2025, a 3 percent year-over-year increase, and employs roughly 3,000 to 4,300 people depending on the data source measured.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is ZoomInfo
paragraphs: ZoomInfo is a B2B go-to-market intelligence platform that provides sales, marketing, and recruiting teams with company and contact data, buyer intent signals, and AI-driven insights to identify and engage prospects.

Despite the similar-sounding name, ZoomInfo is an entirely separate company from Zoom Video Communications, the video conferencing provider. ZoomInfo's roots trace to Eliyon Technologies (founded 2000), later rebranded after merging with DiscoverOrg in 2019, and it is headquartered in Vancouver, Washington.

In May 2025, the publicly traded company changed its NASDAQ ticker from ZI to GTM, reflecting a strategic shift toward positioning itself as a broader go-to-market intelligence platform rather than solely a contact database.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SalesOS, ZoomInfo's flagship product, combines contact and company search with sales engagement automation and workflow tools to power outbound and inbound prospecting.
Conversation intelligence, powered by ZoomInfo's Chorus acquisition, analyzes sales calls to surface coaching insights and deal risk signals.
Intent data and account-based marketing modules help marketing teams identify accounts actively researching relevant topics.
Copilot, ZoomInfo's AI layer, prioritizes accounts and helps generate personalized outreach content based on buyer signals.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ZoomInfo does not publish self-serve pricing; all plans require a custom annual contract negotiated with the sales team, with no monthly billing option.

Industry pricing guides commonly cite SalesOS Professional starting around 14,995 dollars per year, Advanced around 24,995 dollars per year, and Elite at 39,995 dollars per year and above, plus per-seat add-on fees.

Additional modules such as intent data, ABM, Copilot, and Chorus conversation intelligence add to the base contract, with realistic total costs for mid-sized sales teams often landing between 25,000 and 60,000 dollars annually.

Deep integrations with major CRM and sales engagement tools
Established, well-known brand in the sales intelligence category
cons: No public self-serve pricing; requires a custom annual sales contract
Significant per-seat add-on costs can push total spend well above the base package price
No monthly billing option, requiring an annual commitment
Total cost can be prohibitive for small businesses and startups

faqs.question: Is ZoomInfo related to Zoom the video conferencing company?
faqs.answer: No, ZoomInfo is a completely separate company. ZoomInfo is a B2B sales intelligence and data platform, while Zoom is a video conferencing company; they share no ownership.

faqs.question: How much does ZoomInfo cost?
faqs.answer: ZoomInfo requires a custom annual contract; industry estimates put SalesOS Professional around 14,995 dollars per year, Advanced around 24,995 dollars per year, and Elite at 39,995 dollars per year and above, before add-ons.

faqs.question: What is ZoomInfo's ticker symbol?
faqs.answer: ZoomInfo trades on NASDAQ under the ticker GTM, after changing from ZI in May 2025.

faqs.question: What is SalesOS?
faqs.answer: SalesOS is ZoomInfo's flagship product combining contact and company search, sales engagement automation, conversation intelligence, and workflow tools.

faqs.question: Does ZoomInfo offer monthly billing?
faqs.answer: No, ZoomInfo sells exclusively through annual contracts with no monthly billing option.

faqs.question: When was ZoomInfo founded?
faqs.answer: ZoomInfo traces back to Eliyon Technologies, founded in 2000, and took its current name and form after merging with DiscoverOrg in 2019.

faqs.question: Where is ZoomInfo headquartered?
faqs.answer: ZoomInfo is headquartered in Vancouver, Washington.

faqs.question: What is ZoomInfo Copilot?
faqs.answer: Copilot is ZoomInfo's AI layer that prioritizes accounts and helps generate personalized outreach content based on buyer intent signals.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, vendor-neutral OCI-native container image registry delivered as a single stateless binary, backed by CNCF Sandbox status.
enrichment.seo_title: Zot OCI Registry Review: Features and Setup Guide (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Learn about Zot, the open-source OCI-native container registry, its single-binary architecture, key features, and CNCF Sandbox status.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zot (also referenced as ZOT OCI Registry) is an open-source, vendor-neutral, OCI-native container image and artifact registry, developed under the project-zot organization on GitHub and distributed under the Apache 2.0 license. Zot strictly follows the OCI Distribution Specification and OCI Image Specification, storing images purely in OCI format rather than relying on a proprietary internal format, which makes it interoperable with standard tooling like Docker, Podman, and Helm. The project was accepted as a CNCF (Cloud Native Computing Foundation) Sandbox Project on December 13, 2022, and the Linux Foundation holds trademark rights over the project name; Cisco is noted as a sponsor and adopter within the project's ecosystem. Zot is architected as a single, statically built binary with no external dependencies, allowing it to run without elevated system privileges on platforms ranging from cloud servers to edge devices like a Raspberry Pi. Despite its lightweight footprint, Zot bundles a broad set of registry capabilities directly into the binary, including vulnerability and security scanning, authentication (HTTP Basic, LDAP, and HTTP Bearer token), authorization, garbage collection, image deduplication, replication, and caching. It also supports OCI artifacts beyond container images, such as Helm charts, software bill of materials (SBOM) files, and image signatures, positioning it as a general-purpose OCI artifact store rather than a container-only registry. Zot offers two build variants: a full, feature-complete build and a minimal, security-focused build for environments that want the smallest possible attack surface. As an open-source, community-governed project rather than a commercial company, Zot has no founding year, headquarters, company size, or commercial pricing in the traditional sense; it is free to self-host and use under its Apache 2.0 license, with active community support through GitHub, Slack, and project documentation at zotregistry.dev.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zot OCI Registry
paragraphs: Zot is an open-source, vendor-neutral, OCI-native container image and artifact registry, built purely on the OCI Distribution and Image Specifications rather than a proprietary internal format.
The project is a CNCF Sandbox Project, accepted on December 13, 2022, with the Linux Foundation holding trademark rights and Cisco noted as a sponsor and adopter in the project's ecosystem.

Unlike heavier registry platforms that require multiple services or external databases, Zot ships as a single, statically built binary with no external dependencies, making it easy to deploy anywhere from cloud infrastructure to edge devices.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Zot bundles security scanning, authentication, authorization, garbage collection, and deduplication directly into the single binary, avoiding the need for separate add-on services.
It supports OCI artifacts beyond container images, including Helm charts, SBOMs, and image signatures, making it useful as a general artifact store for cloud-native supply chains.
Two build variants are available: a full-featured build and a minimal, security-hardened build for constrained or high-assurance environments.
It works seamlessly with standard container tooling such as Docker pull commands, Podman, runc, and Helm, requiring no changes to existing client workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zot is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license, with no paid tiers or commercial licensing from the project itself.
Because it is self-hosted, actual cost depends on the infrastructure used to run it, ranging from a Raspberry Pi to production cloud servers.
Support is community-driven through GitHub issues, Slack channels, and official documentation rather than a paid support contract.

features.title: OCI Distribution and Image Specification compliance

features.title: Multiple authentication methods

features.description: Choose a feature-complete build or a minimal, security-focused build for smaller attack surface.

pros: Free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Single binary with no external dependencies simplifies deployment
CNCF Sandbox status signals community governance and cloud-native alignment
Broad OCI artifact support beyond just container images
Runs on constrained hardware like a Raspberry Pi as well as production cloud infrastructure
cons: Self-hosted only, requiring the user to manage infrastructure and updates
No commercial support contract or SLA from the project itself
Still in CNCF Sandbox stage, an earlier maturity level than Incubating or Graduated projects
Smaller ecosystem and community size compared to established registries like Docker Hub or Harbor

faqs.question: Is Zot OCI Registry free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zot is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license; costs are limited to whatever infrastructure you use to host it.

faqs.question: What is Zot's relationship to CNCF?
faqs.answer: Zot was accepted as a CNCF Sandbox Project on December 13, 2022, with the Linux Foundation holding trademark rights over the project.

faqs.question: Does Zot work with Docker?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zot supports standard Docker pull and push workflows as well as Podman, runc, and Helm.

faqs.question: What OCI artifacts does Zot support besides container images?
faqs.answer: Zot supports Helm charts, software bills of materials (SBOMs), and image signatures alongside standard container images.

faqs.question: Can Zot run on a Raspberry Pi?
faqs.answer: Yes, its single-binary architecture with no external dependencies allows it to run on lightweight hardware including a Raspberry Pi.

faqs.question: Does Zot include security scanning?
faqs.answer: Yes, vulnerability and security scanning are built directly into the binary rather than requiring a separate service.

faqs.question: What authentication methods does Zot support?
faqs.answer: Zot supports HTTP Basic authentication, LDAP, and HTTP Bearer token authentication.

faqs.question: Who maintains Zot?
faqs.answer: Zot is maintained as an open-source community project under the project-zot GitHub organization, with Cisco noted as a sponsor and adopter, under CNCF Sandbox governance.

enrichment.short_description: Modular B2B event management platform for virtual, hybrid, and in-person conferences, field events, and webinars with CRM-integrated analytics.
enrichment.starting_price: Custom pricing based on event format and attendee volume
enrichment.seo_title: Zuddl Review: Event Management Platform Features and Pricing (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Explore Zuddl's virtual, hybrid, and in-person event platform, including Studio production, booths, networking, CRM integration, and custom pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Zuddl is a modular B2B event management platform for virtual, hybrid, and in-person events, founded in 2020 by Bharath Varma and Vedha Sayyaparaju and headquartered in San Francisco, with additional operations in Bangalore, India. The company went through Y Combinator's 2020 batch and raised a 13.35 million dollar Series A round led by Alpha Wave Incubation and Qualcomm Ventures, with participation from GrowX Ventures and Waveform Ventures, bringing total funding to approximately 15.5 million dollars. Zuddl targets B2B marketers, associations, and conference organizers running events ranging from small webinars to large-scale conferences with 100 to over 25,000 attendees. The platform's Studio module offers broadcast-quality live streaming with multi-speaker sessions, branded overlays, and real-time production control, letting organizers produce TV-like broadcasts without external production crews. For virtual and hybrid events, Zuddl provides customizable exhibitor booths with downloadable content, video chat, and lead capture forms, alongside networking tools such as breakout rooms, one-on-one meeting scheduling, gamification, and AI-powered attendee matchmaking. A core differentiator is deep, bi-directional CRM and marketing automation integration with platforms like Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, and Pardot, syncing attendee engagement and conversion data directly into go-to-market systems. Organizers can fully brand registration pages, virtual event venues, and mobile event apps to match their company identity. Zuddl's pricing is not publicly listed and varies by event format: third-party sources cite starting points such as roughly 80 dollars per month for entry-level use, around 10,000 dollars per year for Zuddl Webinar, and around 12,000 dollars per year for Zuddl Conferences, with virtual event pricing commonly based on annual attendee volume rather than per-event or per-registration fees. Prospective customers generally need to request a custom quote based on their specific event program needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Zuddl
paragraphs: Zuddl is a modular event management platform built for B2B marketers, associations, and conference organizers to run virtual, hybrid, and in-person events, along with standalone webinars, from a single unified system.

Founded in 2020 by Bharath Varma and Vedha Sayyaparaju and headquartered in San Francisco with operations in Bangalore, Zuddl emerged from Y Combinator's 2020 cohort and has raised approximately 15.5 million dollars in funding, including a Series A round led by Alpha Wave Incubation and Qualcomm Ventures.

The platform is designed to combine broadcast-quality production with real-time, bi-directional CRM integration, positioning it as a tool for marketers who need events to feed directly into pipeline and revenue reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The Studio module provides live streaming with multi-speaker sessions, branded overlays, and production controls for TV-quality broadcasts without external crews.
Customizable virtual booths let exhibitors offer downloadable content, video chat, and lead capture forms to attendees.
Networking tools include breakout rooms, one-on-one meeting scheduling, gamification, and AI-powered matchmaking to drive meaningful attendee connections.
Deep CRM and marketing automation integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, and Pardot sync engagement and conversion data back into go-to-market systems.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Zuddl does not publish standardized public pricing; costs vary by event format and require a custom quote from Zuddl's sales team.
Third-party sources cite entry points around 80 dollars per month for lighter usage, roughly 10,000 dollars per year for Zuddl Webinar, and roughly 12,000 dollars per year for Zuddl Conferences.
Virtual event pricing is commonly based on annual attendee volume (for example, 2,000 to 3,000 attendees per year) rather than per-event or per-registration fees.

faqs.question: What is Zuddl used for?
faqs.answer: Zuddl is used to run virtual, hybrid, and in-person B2B events, conferences, field events, and webinars from a single platform.

faqs.question: When was Zuddl founded?
faqs.answer: Zuddl was founded in 2020 by Bharath Varma and Vedha Sayyaparaju.

faqs.question: Where is Zuddl headquartered?
faqs.answer: Zuddl is headquartered in San Francisco, California, with additional operations in Bangalore, India.

faqs.question: How much does Zuddl cost?
faqs.answer: Zuddl does not publish standard pricing; costs vary by event format, with third-party estimates ranging from roughly 80 dollars per month for basic usage to around 10,000 to 12,000 dollars per year for webinar and conference packages.

faqs.question: Does Zuddl integrate with CRM tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zuddl offers bi-directional integration with Salesforce, HubSpot, Marketo, and Pardot.

faqs.answer: Studio is Zuddl's live production module offering broadcast-quality streaming, multi-speaker sessions, and branded overlays.

faqs.question: How many attendees can Zuddl support?
faqs.answer: Zuddl is built to support events ranging from about 100 attendees to more than 25,000 for large-scale conferences.

faqs.question: Has Zuddl raised funding?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zuddl went through Y Combinator's 2020 batch and has raised approximately 15.5 million dollars, including a 13.35 million dollar Series A round led by Alpha Wave Incubation and Qualcomm Ventures.
